channel.title: Safety on Chasing's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Safety on Chasing's blog

item.title: Safety in Systems Programming
item.description: 1. Introduction The gets() function cannot be used securely. Because of its lack of bounds checking, and the inability for the calling program to reliably determine the length of the next incoming line, the use of this function enables malicious users to arbitrarily change a running program’s functionality through a buffer overflow attack. It is strongly suggested that the fgets() function be used in all cases. How can we find and/or prevent problems like this?
